title: Run Keycloak With Docker Compose
description:
## Stand Up a Dev Keycloak

**Your task:** write a Docker Compose file at **`/root/keycloak-compose.yml`** with a service that:
1. uses the official image **`quay.io/keycloak/keycloak`** (any tag)
2. sets the bootstrap admin via **`KEYCLOAK_ADMIN`** and **`KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD`** environment variables
3. publishes a port (e.g. `8080:8080`)

> 💡 For local dev the command is `start-dev`. Validate the YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/keycloak-compose.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Compose service for Keycloak
hints.body: Define one service with the quay.io/keycloak/keycloak image, the start-dev command, the admin env vars, and a published port.
hints.command:
printf 'services:
  keycloak:
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
    command: start-dev
    environment:
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
' > /root/keycloak-compose.yml

validation.description: Checks for a Keycloak service with the admin env var.

commands.description: A keycloak service uses the official image and sets KEYCLOAK_ADMIN
commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml,json; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/keycloak-compose.yml')); svcs=d.get('services',{}); print('ok' if any('keycloak' in str(s.get('image','')).lower() and 'KEYCLOAK_ADMIN' in json.dumps(s.get('environment')) for s in svcs.values()) else 'no')"
